title: Artificial Evorution Labolatory
wikitext:
[]: Artificial Evorution Labolatory             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Dec 07, 2007 1:39 pm Using both versions of the scene in the AEL from the animatic and the aired episode there are some points of interest - In the aired episode we can't make out all the numbers scrawled on the wall because what looks like a I.V. stand is blocking one of them, in the animatic it’s written clearly as 128. Above 128 is a formula written on the wall that is only present in the animatic, the r in the formula may be an i and the u's may be v's or o's, but other than that it's clearly written. Much more obvious in the animatic again is the number 666, which appears as a faded, blurry red stencil in the aired episode. On the far left of the picture is a string of letters: TAGATAGA, this is obviously genetic code, what's written underneath it isn't clear in either version of the episode unfortunately. In the next picture 7rD2 isn't clear in the aired episode as the r is obscured by a piece of equipment, in the animatic it's written over everything else so you can see it. There is probably another one of these D2's just underneath the camera, but we don't get to see it. TOP/BOTTOM and Strangeness (dss)M has already been dealt with before by S.S., it has something to do with quarks. On the far left of the picture is written something-PF, but I cropped the picture too much so you can't make it out in the screenie In the animatic something-XFD is written behind Misato's head, in the aired episode this is replaced with the letters ness from Strangeness. This last bit was brought up by meself over a year ago on this very site! The material that covers the ceiling and falls down to obscure part of 666 (see first picture) has an odd pattern on it that happens to really look like one of the trigrams from the Ye Ching, usually I'd just say it's a coincidence but this trigram is associated with both water and the moon, which matches up with All That Stuff that's tied to Rei throughout the series, and it also corresponds to one of Ritsuko's lines while in the AEL. Right, so the purpose of this post was to point out all the writing on the walls of the AEL clearly and to provide info. for those who brought the platnium thinpack which doesn't have the animatic extras. If anyone can tell us what that formula might be about in the first image or any of the rest of it then plz halp. Oh!, and the title of this thread: that's how Artificial Evolution Laboratory was spelt in the production film!

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Dec 07, 2007 9:32 pm Looks like zuggy is back at the top of his game.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Dec 07, 2007 9:36 pm With anything other than Evangelion , I'd laugh at the attempt to make sense of k'an. But with this show, you really just never know.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Dec 07, 2007 9:48 pm Strangeness, Top and Bottom are Quantum Mechanics terms. That derivative and 7iD2 or whatever that is look like plain junk to me. TiD2 is Titanium Deutride- let me see if this compound is used for anything significant, or if it's been just chucked in there for the heck of it. The trigram find is very interesting- I always wondered what that thing on the cieling was! Edit: A google search shows that TiD2 has been used in small fusion experiments, but other than a bunch of lab experiments, there appears nothing great. Chalk it up to more pedantry.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Dec 07, 2007 10:09 pm oh my goodness :roll: I'm calling fanwank!!

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 07, 2007 11:08 pm ...I thought "Artificial Evolution Laboratory" was the early facility they used for the Evas by Gehirn? Then again that would be in the older, less-used parts of what would go on to become Nerv HQ. ***There are 6 kinds of "Quark". Up/Down, Top/Bottom, Charm/Strange (they are paired). Three Quarks make up a Proton, or a Neutron. Protons are Up Up Down, Neutrons are Up Down Down. The other four kinds don't occur in normal matter as we usually encounter.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Dec 07, 2007 11:17 pm The AEL is like the lower levels of Black Mesa: you don't know they exist until you have to dash through them with an empty gun in your hand and a pack of starving houndeyes on your tail.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Dec 08, 2007 1:20 am Hah, yeah, and your crowbar! WOOT! I just had to say that; I don't know anything else about what we're talking about right now, so I'll just return to the white noise.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Dec 08, 2007 10:56 am Up and Down are regular quarks, seen in normal matter as we know it. Charm and Strange are heavier versions, and Top and Bottom are superheavy. While the fundamental unit of electrical charge is the charge of a single electron, (-1.6 x 10^-19 Coulomb) an Up quark has 2/3rd the charge (+ve) and a Down quark has one third(-ve charge) One Up quark and two Down quarks make a neutron, while two Up and one Down make a proton. Notice that the charges add/subtract nicely, so that a neutron has zero net charge and a proton has a positive charge equal to the negative charge on an electron. An Up quark is heavier than a Down quark, and for that matter Protons are heavier than Neutrons. The other four quarks are created only in particle accelerators and are not found naturally. (The positive Charm and Top quarks are again heavier than their negatively charged counterparts) As far as I understand, there's no point in talking about quarks here, because the heavier quarks, having greater mass, should have less of wave properties than whatever form of matter that the Angels are supposed to be made of.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Dec 08, 2007 1:45 pm Interesting angle that the quarks may be related to Angel Stuff, but you say they would be too heavy to have wave/particle properties - could they be related somehow to the ATF or the soul in NGE then (I know next to nothing about any of this, so forgive my ignorance if that sounds stupid)? 28 + 20 + 50 + 82 + 128 = 308 / 308 x 2 = 616 , which is supposed to be the more 'correct' number rather than 666 in Revelations (which I don't agree with but that ain't important). If the numbers on the tickets went that high I'd so use those as my lottery numbers.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Dec 08, 2007 7:29 pm "This is the light of my soul, a sacred barrier through which none may trespass." "Kaworu, I don't understand what you're talking about." "Lol, quarks."

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 09, 2007 1:37 pm Zugzwang wrote: 28 + 20 + 50 + 82 + 128 = 308 / 308 x 2 = 616 , which is supposed to be the more 'correct' number rather than 666 in Revelations (which I don't agree with but that ain't important).

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Dec 09, 2007 1:48 pm There goes that theory then. UNLESS - Anno has psychic future vision.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Dec 09, 2007 1:50 pm Not so fast, you!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Dec 09, 2007 6:57 pm Zugzwang wrote: There goes that theory then. UNLESS - Anno has psychic future vision.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 09, 2007 9:06 pm The chalkboard in Shinji's classroom already had several math errors (at least according to AMV Hell 4), so it's entirely possible.
